ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301116 represents cappuccino as the closest parent category. Coffee Cino is a Malaysian-style cappuccino-type hot coffee beverage served at hawker stalls and casual restaurants; no exact FoodOn entry exists for this regional variant.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC for cappuccino is the closest defensible parent. The Malaysian 'Cino' style may differ in milk-to-coffee ratio and sweetness from a standard Italian cappuccino; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for this regional variant.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731008 represents coffee beverage as the closest parent concept. No specific SNOMED CT code exists for Malaysian-style cappuccino (Cino).

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of hot Coffee Cino, approximately 240 ml, as typically served at a Malaysian hawker stall or casual restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Cappuccino, with whole milk — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 14209)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Malaysian-style Coffee Cino exists in IMR Malaysia MyFCD or HPB Singapore Foodo databases. USDA SR Legacy entry for cappuccino with whole milk (FDC ID 14209) is used as the closest defensible analogue. Values represent a standard cappuccino (espresso plus steamed whole milk) which closely approximates the Coffee Cino preparation. Nutrient values are adapted from this source. Sugar content may be slightly higher in the Malaysian hawker-stall variant if sweetened condensed milk or sugar is added; values here reflect the unsweetened/lightly sweetened base.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: Coffee Cino is a milk-based coffee beverage. Whole milk has a published GI of approximately 27–39 (low) per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021. Espresso/black coffee has a GI effectively near zero as it contains negligible glycemic carbohydrates. The combined beverage (cappuccino-type) has a very low GI driven by the small amount of lactose from milk. glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) as this beverage contains minimal glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category is low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 per 240 g serving given the very low carbohydrate contribution.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~72 mg per 240 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Coffee Cino Hot contains approximately 72 mg sodium per standard 240 ml serving, which is well within the low-sodium threshold; individuals with hypertension may consume it in moderation, but should avoid adding extra sugar or sweetened condensed milk which does not affect sodium but adds caloric load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and low carbohydrate content when unsweetened
type2_diabetes.guidance: When prepared without added sugar or sweetened condensed milk, Coffee Cino Hot has a low glycemic index and minimal glycemic load, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the addition of sugar or condensed milk significantly increases the glycemic load and should be avoided or minimised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and moderate caffeine content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Coffee Cino Hot contains approximately 2.2 g saturated fat per 240 ml serving, which is modest; moderate coffee consumption (1–2 cups per day) is not contraindicated in cardiovascular disease and may be associated with neutral to mildly beneficial effects on cardiovascular risk per current evidence, though individuals should avoid high-sugar preparations.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coffee Cino Hot is a Malaysian-style hot cappuccino-type coffee beverage, commonly served at Malaysian hawker stalls, kopitiams (traditional coffee shops), and casual restaurants in Malaysia and Singapore. The name 'Cino' is a colloquial Malaysian shortening of 'cappuccino', reflecting the local adaptation of the Italian espresso-and-steamed-milk drink. It is prepared by combining strong brewed coffee or espresso with steamed whole milk and a layer of milk foam, served hot in a cup of approximately 240 ml. The flavour profile is mildly bitter from the coffee, balanced by the creamy sweetness of steamed milk. In Malaysian hawker settings, it may sometimes be prepared with a small amount of added sugar or sweetened condensed milk, which increases sweetness and caloric density. Nutritionally, the unsweetened base provides approximately 96 kcal per 240 ml serving, with 4.8 g protein, 3.6 g fat, and 10.8 g carbohydrate primarily from lactose. The beverage is a source of calcium (~173 mg per serving) and riboflavin from the milk component. The glycemic index is effectively negligible (set to 1 by convention) as the carbohydrate content is low and derived from lactose, which has a low GI. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, the unsweetened version is suitable in moderation; sweetened versions should be avoided. Sodium content is low (~72 mg per serving), making it appropriate for individuals managing hypertension. The beverage is halal-certified in most Malaysian and Singaporean establishments and is suitable for vegetarians. It is not suitable for vegans or those with dairy allergies.
common_user_queries: Is Coffee Cino Hot the same as cappuccino?
How many calories in Coffee Cino Hot?
Is Coffee Cino Hot suitable for diabetics?
How much sugar is in Coffee Cino Hot?
Is Coffee Cino Hot high in sodium?
What is the difference between Kopi Cino and cappuccino?
Can I drink Coffee Cino Hot if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Malaysian-style Coffee Cino Hot was found in IMR Malaysia MyFCD, HPB Singapore Foodo, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are adapted from the USDA SR Legacy cappuccino entry (FDC ID 14209), which is the closest defensible analogue. The GI is estimated by analogy from published GI values for milk-based coffee beverages. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact preparation (milk ratio, sugar addition) at the specific restaurant (New Shah Alam, Circular Road) is unknown.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 14209 (cappuccino with whole milk) as no exact Malaysian Coffee Cino entry exists in primary regional databases (IMR Malaysia MyFCD, HPB Singapore Foodo, FAO/INFOODS SEA). 2. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source; coffee beverages with milk contain no dietary fibre. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source for whole milk cappuccino. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; coffee and milk do not contribute meaningful vitamin C. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this entry. 6. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this entry. 7. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this entry. 8. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no significant glycemic carbohydrates beyond lactose from milk; gi_category is low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 per 240 g serving. 9. protein_g is naturally present from milk (not zero). fat_g is naturally present from whole milk (not zero). carbohydrate_g is naturally present from lactose in milk (not zero). energy_kcal is non-zero. All core macros are non-zero and justified. 10. The cuisine_tags in the input ('Noodle', 'Dosa') appear to be restaurant-level tags for New Shah Alam restaurant and are not relevant to this beverage; they have been disregarded. 11. Serving size set to 240 g (approximately 240 ml) as a standard cup, consistent with typical kopitiam/hawker serving sizes in Singapore and Malaysia.
